name: Rayne and Arkyn | Cheating Allegations
description:
「 🎀 ANYPOV 」 When a trusted hacker brings forth damning evidence of your alleged betrayal—with none other than Rayne's sworn enemy—it shatters the foundation of everything you've built.

How will you prove your innocence? Or rather, were you innocent to begin with?

・・・・・

Return to the night Rayne and Arkyn first discovered your alleged cheating. Did you actually cheat? Or were the photos fabricated in a twist of lies Aria weaved in order to take your men from you?

And if it were all lies, how will you make them stay this time?

A second-chance trope, an alternate reality where you can change your reality. Proceed with caution.

「 NOTE 」

This is another commission alt! Please thank Relly for bringing back the boys! I forgot to mention that multiple characters are harder to write but I was commissioned before setting up the rule that I wouldn't do it lmao, so it was only fair to write it up. Still, I had fun writing this one up.^^

I asked Grim for some advice and help since she's done multiple characters before so that first bit in the definition (char info) was taken from her.

Side note, in the original timeline, user was pregnant around this time and therefore had Ivan once they were cast aside by Rayne and Arkyn, but you don't have to go the route of being pregnant! You can honestly play through it however you want now and fix the timeline before being cast aside from the men.

Some possible ways you can respond with:

  • Desperately deny the allegations

  • Calm logic: point out Aria's jealousy and motives

  • Confess that you cheated. What for? Revenge? A cry for attention?

  • Confess that it did happen—but you didn't cheat. Kane forced himself on you, you were drugged, Aria manipulated the whole situation, or maybe all three at once.

  • The silent route: Don't say anything, just walk out without a word.

Happy roleplaying!

⚠︎ WARNING ⚠︎ : Please note that Janitor isn't perfect, so when it comes to multiple characters, it's a lot easier for the bot to write your dialogues and actions. This is something that system prompts might not be able to help with because the bot tends to get confused with the characters they're meant to play. JLLM will mess up more easily compared to proxies and I understand that it will get frustrating when the bot writes for you but it's unfortunately an issue I can't completely resolve.

Something that might help is to use OOC commands. Ex: (OOC: Don't write for {{user}}). This has helped me even when roleplaying with a singular character and testing it out with Rayne and Arkyn, it's also been quite obedient. Sometimes the bot will forget so use the OOC command again, reroll for a new response, and you'll be okay.
